content: Festering Strikes is a really good move in neutral;
the parry mechanic grants it melee iframes, and it can be used when close to the opponent.
This move can be punished if they block the first 2 hits, but barely anyone can actually do it.
Once you land it, if you time it correctly,
it's possible to follow it up with Chokehold right after the last hit lands, creating a true combo.
festering-strikes.author: Air Putrifer

content: This special variant is very situational,
and I personally never use it, but it can be used to follow up with the special Roach Swarm variant and Chokehold.
fierce-strikes.author: Air Putrifer

content: I mainly use Detach as a poking tool in neutral.
If they don't react to it, either side dash behind them to land a hit or dash away and use Aerial Roach Swarm
(depends on who you think will win the M1 trade).
detach.author: Air Putrifer

content: If they dash away, try curving it to pull them in.
If they block it, it still fills up the injury bar.
You can also curve it on purpose if you think they're going to block it, since it will fill the injury bar more.
This move can also be used to evasive bait, since if you use it on a ragdolled player, you can combo it into Chokehold.
reattach.author: Air Putrifer

content: Chokehold can be used to stun caused by yourself.
This means you can hold block and use it to catch your opponent off guard when M1 trading.
I only recommend doing this when you're certain the opponent won't hold block or will M1.
Other than this, I recommend saving it to follow up after Festering Strikes
or to evasive bait after using Reattach on a ragdolled player.
chokehold.author: Air Putrifer

content: Roach Swarm can be used in neutral and after Reattach since it's unblockable.
Regular variant is better for opponents rushing in on you.
Air variant is for opponents further away you want to rush in on or to evasive bait (you can pair this with Chokehold for a free combo).
The special variant is for combos.
roach-swarm.author: Air Putrifer

content: Currently, Black Death's R special is pretty useless; it deals small damage,
and the green effect it inflicts barely distracts the opponent.
I usually use it from a safe distance and pray it does something
(sometimes it interrupts stuff, but it's very weird).
You will usually see your opponent exploding the insects on purpose to avoid this
(you can't really do anything against that).
You can forcefully explode the insects by yourself too by hitting them with Reattach
or the Festering Strikes special variant;
yet again, this is mid.
earthen-insect-trance.author: Air Putrifer
